title: Pyroscope Deep Dive — Multi-Stack Operations
description: Comprehensive Pyroscope continuous profiling deep-dive dashboard covering write path, read path, storage, and resource utilization across multiple clusters and stacks.

panels.title: Instances Up
panels.description: Total number of Pyroscope instances currently reporting as healthy. A drop indicates instance failures or rolling restarts.

panels.title: Ingestion Rate
panels.description: Aggregate byte ingestion rate across all Pyroscope distributors. Indicates the volume of profiling data being pushed into the system.

panels.title: Profiles Received/s
panels.description: Rate of individual profiling profiles received by the distributor per second. Represents the throughput of the write path entry point.

panels.title: Active Series
panels.description: Total number of active profiling series across all ingesters. High cardinality here may lead to memory pressure and performance degradation.

panels.title: Head Size
panels.description: Total in-memory head block size across all ingesters. Monitors memory consumption of the most recent, not-yet-flushed profiling data.

panels.title: Query Rate
panels.description: Rate of query and render requests hitting the Pyroscope read path. Monitors the demand on the query frontend and querier components.

panels.title: Profiles Received/s by Cluster
panels.description: Rate of profiles received by the distributor, broken down by cluster. Helps identify which clusters are generating the most profiling traffic and detect imbalances.

panels.title: Push Latency P50/P90/P99
panels.description: Latency percentiles for push and ingest routes on the Pyroscope write path. High P99 values indicate tail-latency issues that may affect profiling agents.

panels.title: Bytes Ingested/s
panels.description: Rate of raw bytes ingested into Pyroscope, broken down by cluster. Useful for capacity planning and identifying unexpected spikes in profiling payload sizes.

panels.title: Discarded Profiles/s
panels.description: Rate of profiles discarded by the distributor, broken down by reason. Common reasons include rate limiting, validation errors, and series limit exceeded. Non-zero values require investigation.

panels.title: Query QPS by Status
panels.description: Queries per second on the read path broken down by HTTP status code. A rising proportion of 4xx or 5xx responses signals query failures or upstream issues.

panels.title: Query Latency P50/P90/P99
panels.description: Latency percentiles for query and render routes. High P99 values indicate slow queries that may be scanning excessive data or hitting cold storage.

panels.title: Merge Duration P50/P99
panels.description: Latency percentiles for profile merge operations. Merging combines multiple profile samples for rendering and is often the most expensive part of query execution.

panels.title: Symbols Resolved/s
panels.description: Rate of symbol resolution operations. Symbol resolution translates raw addresses into human-readable function names and file locations. High rates correlate with query volume.

panels.title: Block Store Size
panels.description: Total size of the block store on disk or object storage. Tracks growth over time and helps with capacity planning for long-term profiling data retention.

panels.title: Object Store Ops/s
panels.description: Rate of operations against the object store backend (e.g., S3, GCS) broken down by operation type. Monitors get, put, delete, and list calls to detect bottlenecks or excessive API usage.

panels.title: Compaction Rate
panels.description: Rate of block compaction operations. Compaction merges smaller blocks into larger ones to improve query performance and reduce storage overhead.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: CPU usage for Pyroscope containers, measured in cores. Shows actual container CPU consumption to detect saturation or inefficient resource allocation.

panels.title: Memory Usage
panels.description: Working set memory usage for Pyroscope containers. Monitors resident memory to detect leaks and ensure pods stay within their resource limits.
